rationale: The citing paper discusses Danaher and Dagger (2013) in detail in the literature review, summarizes its specific findings (banner ads increase website visits but not direct offline purchase incidence), and uses it to motivate key unanswered questions. The 2017 study explicitly positions its contribution as addressing gaps left by prior work, including consumer heterogeneity, direct vs indirect pathways, and cross-campaign effects. It also returns to the cited paper in results interpretation (e.g., noting findings are in line with Danaher and Dagger on no direct within-campaign offline effect), indicating an active comparative role rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Danaher and Dagger (2013) by extending the same research stream on online ads' offline consequences to a richer framework with recency-based segments, mediation via website visits, and within- vs cross-campaign dynamics.
